name: Mandarin Egg Roll B1
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the mushrooms in hot water for 15 minutes. Drain, cut off tough stems, and shred to make 1/2 cup.
2. Heat 1/4 cup vegetable oil in a wok over high heat until hot. Add garlic, ginger, and pork. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
3. Add cooking wine and shrimp, and stir-fry for another 2 minutes.
4. Add mushrooms and remaining vegetables, and stir-fry for 2 more minutes.
5. Add sesame seed oil and salt to taste. Mix well and drain excess liquid.
6. Place vegetable mixture aside.
7. Heat the remaining vegetable oil in a clean wok to boiling point (about 400°F).
8. Lay egg roll wrappers on a counter, place about 1/8 of the reserved vegetable mixture on one end of each wrapper.
9. Moisten the edges of the wrapper with egg white and roll tightly, tucking in the ends on both sides.
10. Deep-fry the egg rolls for 5 to 10 minutes until golden brown.
11. Remove from oil and drain on paper towels.
